claims.claimText: Anterior Shoulder Instability pathoanatomy centers on labrum, glenoid, humeral-head and related stabilizers.

metadata.claim_text: Anterior Shoulder Instability pathoanatomy centers on labrum, glenoid, humeral-head and related stabilizers.

claims.claimText: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating anterior shoulder instability.

metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating anterior shoulder instability.

claims.claimText: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for anterior shoulder instability.

metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for anterior shoulder instability.

claims.claimText: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for anterior shoulder instability — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for anterior shoulder instability — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

claims.claimText: Shoulder instability is usually a labral/capsular injury pattern whose recurrence risk is highest in young active patients.

metadata.claim_text: Shoulder instability is usually a labral/capsular injury pattern whose recurrence risk is highest in young active patients.

claims.claimText: First-time anterior dislocation in a young athlete carries high recurrence risk without stabilization.

metadata.claim_text: First-time anterior dislocation in a young athlete carries high recurrence risk without stabilization.

claims.claimText: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for anterior shoulder instability require attending-defined progression gates.

metadata.claim_text: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for anterior shoulder instability require attending-defined progression gates.

decisionPoints.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from anterior shoulder instability despite appropriate initial management
decisionPoints.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

decisionPoints.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after anterior shoulder instability treatment
decisionPoints.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
